themes_heading: Hormuz re-escalates · GPT-5.6 broadly live · Microsoft cuts Anthropic · Bank earnings day
synthesis_md:
The most consequential story remains the **US-Iran ceasefire unraveling**. The June 17 Islamabad Memorandum—which set a 60-day window for permanent peace talks—is under severe pressure: [the US launched its third consecutive night of strikes](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/7/13/us-and-iran-trade-strikes-as-ceasefire-comes-under-growing-strain) against Iranian military targets on July 12-13, while Iran struck two tankers in the Strait of Hormuz. [Brent crude surged 9.6% Monday and extended to ~$84.84 on Tuesday](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/14/oil-prices-today-brent-wti-hormuz-trump-toll-iran.html) as Trump proposed a 20% toll on all Hormuz-transiting cargo—framed as a safe-passage fee but received by markets as fresh escalation. Strait crossings collapsed to just six vessels in an 18-hour window; Pakistan and Qatar are trying to restart negotiations.

On AI, [OpenAI’s GPT-5.6 family](https://thenextweb.com/news/openai-gpt-5-6-broad-rollout-us-approval)—Sol, Terra, and Luna—broadly launched on July 9 after US Commerce Department security clearance, a regulatory first for a major model release. All three carry a 1M-token context window; Terra ($2.50/$15 per million tokens) is positioned as GPT-5.5 quality at half the price. Simultaneously, [Microsoft began routing high-volume Copilot prompts in Excel and Outlook to its in-house MAI models](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-07/microsoft-replaces-openai-anthropic-with-own-ai-in-some-apps), with Mustafa Suleyman stating the goal is to "reduce and ultimately eliminate" Anthropic spend. Frontier tasks stay on external models for now, but the signal is clear.

[Q2 bank earnings season opens today](https://intellectia.ai/blog/q2-2026-bank-earnings-preview-jpmorgan-goldman-sachs)—JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, BofA, Wells Fargo, and Citi all report. Goldman is expected to deliver ~32% YoY EPS growth on trading and advisory revenues, landing against rising two-year Treasury yields and Hormuz-driven inflation fears.

## what this means for you

**Hormuz at $85 Brent**: Norway captures the upside directly—Equinor and the Government Pension Fund benefit from elevated crude. But European gas prices follow, and Norwegian tanker operators (Frontline, Hafnia) face a war-risk premium dilemma. The 60-day negotiating window matters more than today’s price.

**GPT-5.6 + MAI shift**: Terra is the tier to benchmark—GPT-5.5 capability at half the price, 1M context, improved caching. More structurally: Microsoft cutting external inference for commodity tasks signals the value layer is migrating to application logic above the model. Build there.

**[European VC at $24B in Q2](https://news.crunchbase.com/venture/data-funding-ai-ma-up-europe-q2-2026/)**: The Nordics now exceed $500B ecosystem valuation and produce more unicorns per capita than anywhere outside Silicon Valley. Seed conditions look better than at any point since 2021.
english_script:
Good morning. Three stories are driving the day.

The fragile US-Iran ceasefire from June is breaking down. The United States launched its third consecutive night of air strikes against Iranian military targets on July twelfth and thirteenth — coastal surveillance systems, drone facilities, and missile installations. Iran responded by attacking two oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz. President Trump then announced a twenty percent toll on all ships transiting the Strait, framed as a fee for safe passage but received by energy markets as another escalation. Brent crude surged nearly ten percent on Monday, reaching around eighty-five dollars a barrel on Tuesday. Vessel crossings of the Strait collapsed to just six ships in an eighteen-hour window, against a normal rate of eighteen to twenty-two per day. Pakistan and Qatar are working to pull both sides back to the table, but the sixty-day negotiating window from the June memorandum looks increasingly fragile.

In artificial intelligence, OpenAI’s new model family went broadly live on July ninth. The three tiers are called Sol, Terra, and Luna. This was the first time a major AI model required a US government security review before a public launch — the Commerce Department’s AI standards office ran the vetting. Sol is the flagship, priced at five dollars per million input tokens and thirty dollars per million output. Terra runs at about half the price and is designed to match the previous generation’s capability. All three carry a context window of one million tokens. The week’s other AI story is Microsoft: the company has started routing everyday Copilot tasks in Excel and Outlook to its own in-house models, with chief Mustafa Suleyman explicitly saying the goal is to reduce and ultimately eliminate payments to Anthropic and OpenAI. Complex tasks remain on external models for now.

Finally, today is the first major day of second-quarter earnings season in the United States. JPMorgan Chase, Goldman Sachs, Bank of America, Wells Fargo, and Citigroup all report before the opening bell. Analysts expect Goldman Sachs to deliver earnings growth of around thirty-two percent year over year, driven by trading revenues and investment banking fees. The results land against a backdrop of rising Treasury yields and fresh inflation concerns tied to the situation in the Strait of Hormuz.

That is your briefing for July fourteenth. Stay sharp.
norwegian_script:
God morgen. Tre historier dominerer dagen.

Den skjøre amerikanske-iranske våpenhvilen fra juni er i ferd med å bryte sammen. USA gjennomførte sin tredje påfølgende natt med luftangrep mot iranske militærmål den tolvte og trettende juli — kystørvernåkingssystemer, dronefasiliteter og missilinstallasjoner. Iran svarte med å angripe to oljetankere i Hormuzstredet. Deretter annonserte president Trump en tjue prosent avgift på alle skip som passerer gjennom stredet — presentert som betaling for trygg passasje, men tolket av energimarkedene som en ny eskalering. Brentoljeprisen steg nesten ti prosent mandag til rundt åttifire til åttifem dollar per fat tirsdag. Skipstrafikken gjennom stredet kollapset til kun seks fartøy på atten timer, mot normalt atten til tjueto daglig. Pakistan og Qatar arbeider med å bringe partene tilbake til forhandlingsbordet, men det seksti dager lange forhandlingsvinduet fra juniavtalen ser stadig mer sårbart ut.

Innen kunstig intelligens gikk OpenAIs nye modellfamilie bredt ut den niende juli. De tre nivåene heter Sol, Terra og Luna. Dette er første gang en stor AI-modell har krevd godkjenning fra den amerikanske regjeringen — fra handelsdepartementets AI-standardskontor — før offentlig lansering. Sol er flaggskipet, priset til fem dollar per million input-tokens og tretti dollar per million output-tokens. Terra koster omtrent det halve og er designet for å matche forrige generasjons kapasitet. Alle tre har et kontekstvindu på én million tokens. Ukens andre AI-sak er Microsoft: selskapet har begynt å rute enkle Copilot-forespørsler i Excel og Outlook til egne interne modeller, og toppsjef Mustafa Suleyman sier eksplisitt at målet er å redusere og til slutt eliminere betalingene til Anthropic og OpenAI. Krevende oppgaver håndteres foreløpig fortsatt av eksterne modeller.

Til slutt: i dag er den første store dagen i andrekvartalets resultatssesong i USA. JPMorgan Chase, Goldman Sachs, Bank of America, Wells Fargo og Citigroup legger alle frem tall før børsåpning. Analytikerne venter at Goldman Sachs leverer om lag trettito prosent inntjeningsvekst sammenlignet med samme kvartal i fjor, drevet av handelsresultater og rådgivningshonorarer. Tallene presenteres mot en bakgrunn av stigende amerikanske statsrenter og ny inflasjonsfrykt knyttet til situasjonen i Hormuzstredet.

Det var din morgenbriefing for tirsdag fjortende juli. Ha en god dag.

Oil gains over 2% as Mideast tensions and Hormuz toll prospects raise supply worries
Oil prices jump as US and Iran trade attacks over Strait of Hormuz

GPT-5.6 pricing: Sol, Terra and Luna tiers explained

https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/7/13.title: US and Iran trade strikes as ceasefire comes under growing strain
https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/7/13.excerpt: The United States launched its third consecutive night of air strikes against Iranian military targets on July 12-13, while Iran struck two oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz in retaliation. The attacks further undermine the June 17 Islamabad Memorandum, which set a 60-day window for permanent peace negotiations. Maritime crossings of the Strait collapsed to just six vessels in an 18-hour window, against a normal rate of 18-22 per day. Pakistan and Qatar are attempting to broker a return to negotiations.

https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/14/oil-pric.title: Oil gains over 2% as Mideast tensions and Hormuz toll prospects raise supply worries
https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/14/oil-pric.excerpt: Oil prices rose on Tuesday after President Trump announced plans to impose a 20% toll on all cargo transiting the Strait of Hormuz. Brent crude extended gains to around $84.84 per barrel following a 9.6% surge on Monday. Two-year Treasury yields reached their highest level since early 2025 as investors priced in greater inflation risks and a potential return to tighter monetary policy.

https://thenextweb.com/news/openai-gpt-5.title: OpenAI wins US clearance for a broad GPT-5.6 rollout after weeks of government testing
https://thenextweb.com/news/openai-gpt-5.excerpt: OpenAI broadly released its GPT-5.6 family on July 9 after the US Department of Commerce completed a security review — the first time a major model required government clearance before wide release. The three tiers, Sol ($5/$30 per million tokens), Terra ($2.50/$15), and Luna ($1/$6), all carry a 1M-token context window and improved prompt caching with a 30-minute minimum cache life.

https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/.title: Microsoft Replaces OpenAI, Anthropic With Own AI in Some Apps
https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/.excerpt: Microsoft began routing high-volume, low-complexity Copilot prompts in Excel and Outlook to its internally built MAI models, reducing reliance on OpenAI and Anthropic for routine tasks such as summarizing emails and formatting spreadsheets. AI chief Mustafa Suleyman stated the goal is to reduce and ultimately eliminate Anthropic spend. Frontier and complex tasks remain on external models.

https://intellectia.ai/blog/q2-2026-bank.title: Q2 2026 Bank Earnings Preview: JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs & Major Banks Report July 14
https://intellectia.ai/blog/q2-2026-bank.excerpt: JPMorgan Chase and Goldman Sachs headline a major earnings day on July 14, with BofA, Wells Fargo, and Citi also reporting. Goldman Sachs is expected to deliver approximately 32% year-over-year EPS growth fueled by blockbuster trading and advisory revenues. JPMorgan consensus EPS sits at $5.44-$5.61, roughly 10% YoY growth. Results arrive against rising two-year Treasury yields and Hormuz-driven inflation concerns.

https://news.crunchbase.com/venture/data.title: Europe Posted Its Strongest Venture Funding Quarter In 4 Years As UK Gains, M&A Holds Up
https://news.crunchbase.com/venture/data.excerpt: European startups raised $24 billion in Q2 2026, up roughly a third quarter over quarter and two-thirds higher than Q2 2025. UK startups led with over $10 billion, the third-largest UK funding quarter on record. The Nordics recorded more unicorns per capita than anywhere outside Silicon Valley, with total ecosystem valuation surpassing $500 billion as AI continues to dominate fundraising.

song_suggestion.title: Strange Days
song_suggestion.why: Morrison's 1967 portrait of disorientation and fracture fits the texture of today exactly: a ceasefire that isn't, an AI model needing government clearance before launch, a tech giant cutting its own AI suppliers. Strange days, indeed.
